core_mechanism: an exact second-order (Hessian/curvature) identity equating the local curvature of one nonlinear scalar functional, evaluated along a one-parameter flow generated by a Hermitian operator, to a Fisher-information sensitivity metric, so that the tangent-space geometry of one quantity controls initial growth, periodic revivals, and stability of extrema of the other
brief_reason: The abstract states that at a reference (stabilizer) state the curvature of a nonlinear entropy functional under an arbitrary Hermitian generator equals the quantum Fisher information up to normalization, and that this same tangent geometry fixes initial growth, revival lifting, and stability of minima.
